D9 BiologyL4 AnalyzeReference

Snake Thermal Pit — Infrared Eye

pit vipers, boas, and pythons have a heat-sensitive pit between eye and nostril — TRPA1 ion channels in the membrane fire at 5 mK above ambient, building a thermal image of warm prey

The pit organ is a tympanic membrane (~15 µm thick) stretched across a cavity. TRPA1 ion channels in the membrane fire at temperatures ~5 mK above ambient — extraordinary thermal sensitivity. Different temperatures trigger different firing rates; the brain reconstructs a coarse thermal image fused with the optic input. Used to ambush warm prey in total darkness.

Drag prey temperature. Thermal contrast signal grows; the snake's "thermal vision" panel overlays the warm body.
